content.full_text:
California 
fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Michael Berman 
Assistant Treasurer 
Voter Guide 
August 17, 1988 
291 South La Cienega Boulevard, Suite 200 
Los Angeles, CA 
90211 
Dear Mr. Berman: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-88-283 
This is in reply to your letter dated July 18, 1988, and to 
confirm our telephone conversations concerning reporting 
expenditures in connection with Voter Guide slate mailers. 
We advised you that, for the semi-annual statements 
covering activity in 1988, it is acceptable for candidates and 
committees which make payments to a slate mailer organization for 
inclusion on a slate mailer to attach to their campaign disclosure 
statements a copy of the organization's expenditure schedules 
(Schedules B and B-1 of the Form 401) . 
Commission staff is reviewing this issue to determine 
whether there is a need for legislative amendment or a regulation. 
However, because of the press of other business, it is unlikely that 
it will be resolved before the end of the year. 
If you have any questions, please call me at (916) 322-5662. 
Sincerely,

California

Michael Berman 
Voter Guide 
July 21, 1988 
1435 South La Cienega Blvd., Ste. 101 
Los Angeles, CA 
90035 
Re: 
88-283 
Dear Mr. Berman: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on July 20, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try.to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. 
Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

sections.question_synthetic: What is the proper method for candidates and committees to report expenditures when making payments to a slate mailer organization for inclusion on a slate mailer?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that candidates and committees may attach copies of the slate mailer organization's expenditure schedules (Schedules B and B-1 of Form 401) to their campaign disclosure statements for semi-annual statements covering 1988 activity.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains both an initial acknowledgment letter (July 21, 1988) and the substantive advice letter (August 17, 1988). The OCR quality is moderate with some garbled text at bottom of pages. No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections with headers, but the substance is clear from letter content. The FPPC notes it is reviewing the issue for potential regulatory amendment.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What is the proper method for candidates and committees to report expenditures when making payments to a slate mailer organization for inclusion on a slate mailer?

CONCLUSION: We advised you that, for the semi-annual statements covering activity in 1988, it is acceptable for candidates and committees which make payments to a slate mailer organization for inclusion on a slate mailer to attach to their campaign disclosure statements a copy of the organization's expenditure schedules (Schedules B and B-1 of the Form 401).

Commission staff is reviewing this issue to determine whether there is a need for legislative amendment or a regulation. However, because of the press of other business, it is unlikely that it will be resolved before the end of the year. If you have any questions, please call me at (916) 322-5662. embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter responding to Michael Berman's inquiry about reporting expenditures for payments to a slate mailer organization. The FPPC confirms its prior telephone advice that candidates and committees may attach the slate mailer organization's expenditure schedules to their disclosure statements.
